codeReview.summary: The scaffold commit creates the requested directories, env template, gitignore entries, and importable src package, but it does not fully satisfy the dependency contract because pyproject.toml replaces the required `pybaseball>=2.3` spec with an unpinned GitHub checkout that the handoff says resolves to version 2.2.7.

issues.description: The feature contract explicitly requires `pybaseball>=2.3`, but the committed dependency is `pybaseball @ git+https://github.com/jldbc/pybaseball.git` instead of the requested versioned requirement. The worker handoff also notes this checkout currently builds as version 2.2.7, so the scaffold is not contract-compliant and installs are not reproducible.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The data-worker procedure assumes a runnable repo and mandatory red-phase TDD, which does not fit first-pass project scaffolding. This feature had to deviate on environment init, baseline validation, and TDD because the repo initially lacked pyproject/pytest/scaffold files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\data-worker\SKILL.md:23-27 requires tests-first/red-phase; C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T14-17-20-455Z__t1-project-scaffolding__b0b77d0b-b2e4-465d-9469-59489787846a.json:87-102 records justified deviations for init, baseline validation, and TDD.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared command manifest still advertises Windows-fragile setup/check commands even though the environment notes now document that Git Bash could not reliably run `.factory/init.sh` and that direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m ...` commands are preferred.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\services.yaml:5-9 still uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"` and globbed `python -m py_compile src/**/*.py`; C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\init.sh:20-24 shells into activation then calls bare `pip`; C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md:54-57 documents the Windows shell gotcha.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission/shared library does not capture that the requested `pybaseball>=2.3` dependency was unavailable on PyPI, forcing the worker to research alternatives and fall back to GitHub HEAD. That missing note leaves future workers with the same requirement ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\features.json:754-765 requires `pybaseball>=2.3`; C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T14-17-20-455Z__t1-project-scaffolding__b0b77d0b-b2e4-465d-9469-59489787846a.json:81-83 says PyPI did not provide that version and the chosen dependency resolves to 2.2.7; C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\worker-transcripts.jsonl:1 includes WebSearch queries for PyPI/GitHub pybaseball version discovery.

summary: Fail: the scaffold shape and validations are present, but the committed dependency spec does not satisfy the feature's explicit `pybaseball>=2.3` requirement. I also noted shared-state gaps around scaffolding-specific skill guidance, Windows-safe command manifests, and undocumented pybaseball version availability.
